rationale: In the citing PDF, Iyer and Villas-Boas (2003) appears in key conceptual and modeling passages rather than only in a generic bibliography-style list. The paper links its bilateral channel bargaining setup and cross-negotiation externality logic to prior channel bargaining work, including this source, and cites it again when positioning the contribution against double-marginalization studies. It is also used to support assumptions about bargaining structure and observability/timing traditions in this literature. So, despite grouped citations, this reference plays a meaningful framing and methodological foundation role.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Iyer and Villas-Boas (2003) as an important prior channel-bargaining foundation for its model setup and literature positioning. It builds its smart-contract analysis on a bargaining tradition that this cited work represents, especially around channel pricing interactions and double marginalization.
